events.summary: New Mexico lawmakers said they are demanding documents from an array of government and private institutions as the first major step in their effort to tell the full story of what Jeffrey Epstein did in the state.

events.title: Bernie Sanders defends Graham Platner after reports of sexually explicit text messages
events.summary: Other progressive members of Congress who endorsed Platner, the leading Democratic candidate for the Senate in Maine, are also coming to his defense.

events.title: Scott Pelley accuses CBS News’ Bari Weiss of ‘murdering’ ‘60 Minutes’
events.summary: “60 Minutes” correspondent Scott Pelley accused CBS News Editor-in-Chief Bari Weiss of “murdering” the venerable newsmagazine at a staff meeting with Nick Bilton, the technology journalist tapped to oversee the show, according to an audio recording and a source who was in the room.

events.title: Hurricane season begins, with the first below-average forecast in a decade
events.summary: Hurricane season in the Atlantic kicked off Monday, and for the first time in more than a decade, forecasters expect it to be slower than usual.

events.title: State trooper guards and daily fever checks: Some hantavirus cruise passengers go home
events.summary: Five of the 18 U.S. cruise ship passengers quarantined in Nebraska went home Monday, halfway through their quarantine after being exposed to hantavirus, the CDC said.

events.title: Trump admin outlines who would be exempt from Medicaid work requirements
events.summary: Pregnant women, parents of young children, veterans with disabilities and several other groups will be exempt from Medicaid’s new work requirements, the Trump administration said Monday

events.title: Teen horse competitor accused of hurting animals during Las Vegas event
events.summary: A teenage competitor is accused of injuring three horses with a knife at a barn near a Las Vegas competition over the weekend, police and horse show organizers said

events.title: Democrats zero in on an anti-corruption message: From the Politics Desk
events.summary: In today’s edition, Allan Smith and Natasha Korecki explore how Democrats are trying to turn the GOP’s “drain the swamp” message on its head.

events.title: 30 gallons of fuel spilled on National Mall after event for America’s 250th birthday
events.summary: More than 30 gallons of fuel spilled onto the National Mall from generators that were used during the recent Freedom 250 event.

events.title: Trump admin appears to back off $1.8B ‘anti-weaponization’ fund after rare GOP backlash
events.summary: The Trump administration signaled it is backing off on creating a $1.8 billion “anti-weaponization” fund announced by the Justice Department that could send money to allies of President Donald Trump.

events.title: 12-year-old boy in critical condition after baseball strikes head during warmups
events.summary: 12-year-old New Jersey boy Xavier Taylor is in critical condition after being struck in the head during warmups before a baseball game last week.

events.title: Democrats think their secret sauce in 2026 is targeting Trump and GOP on ‘corruption’
events.summary: Pennsylvania Gov. Josh Shapiro decried corruption in his primary night address to supporters in Bucks County last month.

events.title: Kash Patel’s girlfriend sues MS NOW, accusing it of pushing ‘recklessly false allegations’
events.summary: Kash Patel’s girlfriend has sued MS NOW, accusing it of using “sham” anonymous sources to “push knowingly or recklessly false allegations.”

events.title: Tehran suspends talks with U.S. over Israeli attacks in Lebanon, Iranian media reports
events.summary: Iran suspended talks with the U.S. to protest Israel’s expanding offensive in Lebanon, according to government-aligned media.

events.title: Brazil and Italy rule out Ebola in previously suspected cases
events.summary: The World Health Organization’s leader traveled to the epicenter of the Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of the Congo over the weekend, as suspected cases and deaths continue to mount.

events.title: Graphic: Track U.S., state and county gas prices
events.summary: Are gas prices declining? NBC News is tracking national, state and county prices at the pump.

events.title: Map: See how gas prices have changed in the county where you live
events.summary: How much gas prices have increased during the Iran war depends entirely on where you live, an NBC News analysis of average gas price data from AAA shows.

events.title: U.S. and Iran trade strikes amid talks to end war
events.summary: The U.S. military said it carried out what it called self-defense strikes on Iranian radar and drone control sites over the weekend, while Iran said it targeted an air base used in the U.S. attack as the two countries continued to trade strikes amid talks to end their war

events.title: Iran official says Trump is stalling talks with ‘excessive demands’
events.summary: President Donald Trump has yet to decide on a deal that could extend the ceasefire with Iran, with no announcement Friday following a Situation Room meeting where he said he would make a “final determination.”

events.title: BTS fans pray to Taoist God of Love in hopes of getting concert tickets
events.summary: BTS fans in Taiwan are seen praying to the Taoist love deity, as tickets for the Taiwan show begin presale this week. It comes fresh off BTS's big win at the American Music Awards last week. NBC News' Jesse Kirsch reports.

events.title: Race against time to find and rescue two men in Laos Cave
events.summary: Rescue teams are rushing to find the two men still trapped in that cave in Laos after five others were rescued over the last few days. And now, there are new signs the two still missing may be alive. NBC News’ Janis Mackey Frayer has the latest.

events.title: U.K. bars left-wing influencers from entering country ahead of speaking gigs
events.summary: The progressive political commentators Cenk Uygur and Hasan Piker said they had been barred from entering the United Kingdom to speak at events. Uygur, who co-hosts the YouTube series “The Young Turks,” and his nephew Piker, who broadcasts his commentary on the video platform Twitch, said on social media they had been blocked because of their views about Israel. NBC News' Sahil Kapur reports.

events.title: Dozens dead after explosives detonate in Myanmar rebel town
events.summary: Dozens were killed after the unexplained detonation of explosives stored by one of Myanmar's rebel armies, the militia that controls the village said.

events.title: Trump defrosts relations with Europe’s last dictator, Alexander Lukashenko
events.summary: The man known as Europe’s last dictator may soon be coming in from the cold as President Donald Trump helps him emerge from years of isolation.

events.title: From solar to charcoal, Cubans look for alternatives amid outages and fuel shortages
events.summary: Some Cubans who’ve been able to buy solar panels say it’s a game changer, but most rely on other alternatives including charcoal, despite health concerns and the steep cost.

events.title: Absence of USAID likely slowed Ebola detection and response, former officials say
events.summary: The Trump administration hollowed out USAID last year, but the State Department denied the changes have hampered its response.

events.title: Rescuers race to save last two men trapped in Laos cave
events.summary: With their elbows and knees scraping the walls as they wriggle through claustrophobic tunnels, rescuers are in a race against time to save two men still stuck in a cave in central Laos.

events.title: Tracking the Ebola outbreak: Where the deadly virus is spreading
events.summary: Learn more about the deadly 2026 Ebola outbreak, including how many cases there are so far, which countries are most affected, how this outbreak compares with others in severity and more.

events.title: Red stripes in Welsh cave declared Britain’s oldest art after being dismissed as a natural phenomenon
events.summary: Dismissed as a natural phenomenon for more than a century, red stripes on a rock in Wales have been found to be the oldest known prehistoric art in Britain.

events.title: French navy seizes Russia-linked oil tanker in high seas, Macron says
events.summary: The French Navy on Sunday boarded an oil tanker, named the Tagor, which was subject to international sanctions and sailing from Russia, French President Emmanuel Macron wrote on X.

events.title: Colombia’s Petro sows doubt about election as favored successor heads to runoff against pro-Trump rival
events.summary: Colombia’s outgoing president sowed doubt Sunday about his country’s elections, which showed his preferred candidate, Iván Cepeda, headed to a runoff next month against right-wing opponent Abelardo de la Espriella

events.title: More than 40 dead in Ebola outbreak in DR Congo and Uganda
events.summary: Over 40 people have died in a growing Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of the Congo and Uganda, where government and international aid organizations are struggling to cope with the spread of the disease

events.title: Netanyahu vows to expand Israel’s grip on Lebanon after deepest incursion in 26 years
events.summary: Israeli forces have crossed the Litani River and captured a strategic mountain in Lebanon, marking the deepest incursion in 26 years.

events.title: Four more men rescued from flooded cave after becoming trapped while searching for gold
events.summary: Five men have now been freed after spending more than a week trapped in a flooded cave in Laos, according to rescuers, with two others still missing

events.title: Spain is legalizing half a million immigrants, a very different policy from the U.S.
events.summary: Nariola Romo, 34, and her family immigrated to Spain from Colombia, but that wasn’t their initial plan.

events.title: Man rescued after more than a week trapped in flooded cave in Laos
events.summary: Rescuers have begun removing miners trapped in a flooded cave in Laos for more than a week, with video showing the efforts as they ran late into the night local time.

events.title: Americans who get Ebola will go to Europe for treatment, not U.S., officials say
events.summary: If additional Americans contract Ebola and need medical care, they will be sent to Europe rather than brought home to the U.S., senior administration officials said.

events.title: Russia denounced after drone hits Romanian apartment building
events.summary: A Russian drone crashed into an apartment building in Romania early Friday, injuring two people and setting the building ablaze, exactly the type of spillover from the war in Ukraine that many in Europe have long feared
